description: This figure presents a schematic diagram illustrating the thermal treatment process for a low-density δ-TRIP steel plate via an intercritical quenching-annealing route. The processing sequence includes: initial homogenization at 1100°C for 2 h, followed by hot rolling and air cooling to room temperature (designated as the HR sample); subsequent reheating to 1050°C (within the ferrite-austenite dual-phase region) for 1 h and water quenching (designated as the IQ-1050 sample); and final annealing treatment at various intercritical temperatures including 620°C and 800°C (with additional temperatures of 680°C and 740°C mentioned in the caption) for 1 h, followed by air cooling to room temperature (yielding samples QA-620, QA-680, QA-740, and QA-800). The diagram marks the critical temperature Ac1 = 617°C, indicating that all annealing treatments were conducted in the intercritical region. This schematic establishes the complete fabrication protocol from ingot to final heat-treated states, providing the experimental basis for investigating the effects of annealing temperature on microstructure and mechanical properties.
